business.title: US stocks today: US market ends lower as semiconductor stocks reverse earlier gains
business.summary: The S&P 500 closed lower as chip stocks like Intel and Arm Holdings retreated, while U.S.-Iran peace talk uncertainty impacted the broader market. Despite a strong quarter driven by AI and tech rallies, concerns over chip supply and geopolitical tensions tempered investor sentiment. Oil prices edged down, though Nvidia and Microsoft saw gains.

business.title: US Fed's Hammack says rates likely on hold 'for quite some time' amid elevated inflation
business.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack indicated interest rates will remain steady for an extended period due to significant economic uncertainty. She expressed concern about an "inflationary mindset" becoming entrenched, noting that consumers and businesses have experienced a decade's worth of inflation in just five years.

business.title: US market today: Citigroup targets stronger profitability as CEO Fraser drives overhaul
business.summary: Citigroup unveiled ambitious profitability targets for 2027-2028, aiming for 11-13% adjusted return on tangible common equity, with further growth expected by 2031. CEO Jane Fraser highlighted a company-wide overhaul and announced a $30 billion share buyback plan, signaling a focus on consistent performance and organic growth, particularly in wealth management.

business.title: US market today: Blackstone, BlackRock cut value of their private credit funds
business.summary: Blackstone and BlackRock asset management firms reported declines in their private credit fund values for the first quarter. This reduction was attributed to markdowns on loans to struggling companies, particularly in the software sector, with concerns over AI's impact on business models cited as a contributing factor.

business.title: India and UAE review full spectrum of Comprehensive Strategic Partnership during FS Misri’s visit
business.summary: Misri also met senior UAE leadership to explore deeper economic engagement.

business.title: West Bengal assembly dissolved after completion of full term
business.summary: The move marks the formal end of the outgoing legislative assembly after the recently concluded electoral process

business.title: AIADMK leader exits Rajya Sabha after winning assembly seat in Mailam
business.summary: The move has sparked widespread political speculation in Tamil Nadu, with discussions around possible shifts in alliances involving AIADMK, DMK, and Tamizhaga Vettri Kazhagam

business.title: US stocks today: Paine Schwartz-backed Suja Life's shares fall 14% in Nasdaq debut
business.summary: Suja Life, an organic juice maker, saw its shares drop 14.3 percent on its Nasdaq debut. The company's valuation settled at $695.3 million. This IPO marks a revival in the consumer space. Investors are selective with capital allocation. Suja Life offers cold-pressed juices and functional sodas. PepsiCo's acquisition of Poppi highlights big players' interest in healthy drinks.

business.title: US stocks today: Space analytics firm HawkEye 360 valued at $3.15 billion after NYSE debut
business.summary: HawkEye 360's stock surged 30% on its Thursday debut, reaching a $3.15 billion valuation after raising $416 million in its U.S. IPO. The defense-tech firm, which uses satellites for signals intelligence, saw strong investor demand. This successful listing arrives as IPO activity is expected to accelerate, with potential SpaceX filings also on the horizon.

business.title: Zerodha’s Rs 7,400 crore MTF book highlights retail shift from cash trading to leveraged bets: Nithin Kamath
business.summary: India's retail trading has shifted towards leveraged products like Margin Trading Facility (MTF) since the pandemic, with cash market activity seeing slower growth. Zerodha's MTF book has surged to nearly Rs 7,400 crore, driven by increased appetite for leveraged positions. Traders are cautioned to consider brokerage costs alongside interest rates, as these can significantly impact returns on leveraged trades.

business.title: Bharat Forge Q4 results: Cons PAT falls 17% YoY to Rs 233 crore, revenue up 18%
business.summary: Bharat Forge reported a 17% year-on-year fall in net profit to Rs 233 crore for the March-ended quarter, despite an 18% revenue growth to Rs 4,528 crore. The company recommended a final dividend of Rs 6.50 per equity share. Despite demand challenges, Bharat Forge secured significant new orders, particularly in defence.

business.title: Nationwide ban soon on herbicide Paraquat Dichloride over toxicity concerns, health risks
business.summary: Experts linked the herbicide to fatal poisoning, kidney failure, lung fibrosis and Parkinson’s disease; proposed ban could disrupt a large agrochemical market and farming practices
